# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-20

An independent investigation identified roughly 10,000 GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware, undetected for over a year.

Went through 14 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
   Directly actionable: vet provenance before cloning or running random GitHub repos, tools, or MCP servers — a routine part of a solo builder's workflow.

2. Google Workspace threatening to block Firefox access
   A platform forcing browser choice on Workspace accounts is a real, immediate decision point for anyone using Firefox with a managed Google account.

3. Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
   A first-party protocol-level change directly relevant to anyone building or deploying MCP servers — squarely in scope for ongoing MCP/agent work.

4. Datasette Apps: Host custom HTML applications inside Datasette
   Concrete, usable feature for Datasette users wanting lightweight internal tools without separate hosting.

5. The design of littlefs
   High-quality technical reference for embedded-systems work, though narrow in scope.

Full brief with all 14 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-20

Draft status: not approved for posting.
